audit_items.proof_trace: Neither caller (binary_rpc_request.cc:91, rpc_client_impl.cc:431) NULL-checks the return value before passing it to ParseFromZeroCopyStream(is.get()). The sole protection is SCHECK(false) in the default branch, which with the default log handler calls abort() — a DoS when an attacker sends an unrecognized compress_type. If a custom log handler is installed without a fatal abort (via the public set_log_handler() API), SCHECK(false) becomes a no-op, execution reaches return NULL at line 34, and ParseFromZeroCopyStream(NULL) causes NPD.
audit_items.attack_scenario: Attacker sends an RPC request with compress_type set to an unrecognized integer (e.g., 5); with the default handler this triggers abort() crashing the server process (DoS). With a custom log handler that omits abort, get_compressed_input_stream() returns NULL, which is passed directly to ParseFromZeroCopyStream(NULL) causing a null pointer dereference and crash.
